tool.name: StreamNetwork

param.name: dem
param.dialogReference: Hydro-conditioned DEM. See tool Terrain Modification/Burn Culverts into DEM to create one

param.name: toggle
param.expression: Existing Stream Lines | Watershed Size Threshold
param.dialogReference: Select whether to find stream initiation points from an existing stream line layer or by setting a watershed size threshold beyond which streams initiate

param.name: stream
param.dialogReference: End points of the supplied stream lines will be used to define the headwaters of the output streams instead of an accumulation threshold

param.name: threshold
param.dialogReference: The Watershed Size Threshold specifies the contributing area above which the model assumes concentrated flows are considered streams

param.name: keep
param.dialogReference: Stream line fields to spatially join with output feature class

param.name: size_bool
param.displayname: Include calculated watershed size as attribute
param.dialogReference: If selected the output feature class will contain a field "Watershed Size (Units)", giving the max watershed size of each stream segment in the area units of the threshold parameter "Stream Initiation Threshold".

param.name: unit
param.expression: {Square US Survey Miles | Unknown | Square International Inches | Square US Inches | Square International Feet | Square US Feet | Square International Yards | Square US Yards | International Acres | US Survey Acres | Square Statute Miles | Square Millimeters | Square Centimeters | Square Decimeters | Square Meters | Square Kilometers | Ares | Hectares}
param.dialogReference: The areal unit to give to the output watershed size attribute column

tool.summary:
This tool takes a hydro-conditioned DEM and derives hydrologically correct streamlines from the DEM.

If mapped streamlines are supplied then the derived streamlines will be limited to initiating at the initiation points of the mapped streams.

If no mapped streamlines are supplied then the streamlines will be derived by the initiation threshold, which specifies the contributing area above which the model assumes concentrated flows are considered streams
